mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand as discount rates compress and real yields turn negative, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 500K persons signals robust labor market recovery and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise and recession risk declines.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) in months with PAYEMS growth > 500K versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move together as leading indicators of consumer demand. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 days due to improved earnings visibility and reduced recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444,926M and HOUST at 1,135K as of 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS growth × HOUST growth) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window, or joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals by > 5% in Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month signal supply-side weakness and potential deflationary pressure. This triggers equity market pessimism as profit margins compress and growth expectations fall, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC > -0.02 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% signals slack labor market and weak consumer confidence. When UNRATE rises above this threshold, equity investors reduce growth expectations and shift to defensive positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 5.5%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate move in opposite directions: when CPI growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains accommodative, real rates fall further, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.98 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Interaction term (CPI deceleration × FEDFUNDS accommodation) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or fails to outperform single-series models by > 8% in out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows positive trend acceleration. When annualized GDP growth exceeds 2.5%, equity earnings expectations rise and forward returns are positive over 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-dependent: it fails in late-cycle overheating.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% versus baseline, or mechanism reverses (negative correlation) in late-cycle periods (UNRATE < 3.5%).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both accelerate month-over-month simultaneously, signaling broad-based economic momentum. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit growth and employment gains reinforce bullish sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.10 and PAYEMS at 141,679K as of 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Interaction term (INDPRO growth × PAYEMS growth) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window, or joint signal underperforms single-series signals by > 10% in Sharpe ratio over any 2-year period.
